name: Beef Loaf (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (moderate). Have four 8x8x2-inch baking pans ready. Do not line pans for this recipe.
2. Mix eggs and milk in a bowl.
3. Add remaining ingredients except meat, and beat well.
4. Mix in ground beef lightly.
5. Divide the mixture into four parts.
6. Press each part into a baking pan. Pack mixture tightly to avoid air pockets.
7. Bake 1 hour or until loaf is done.
To Serve Without Freezing:
Serve immediately after removing from the oven.
To Freeze:
1. Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature.
2. Remove from pans and place on freezer wrap, allowing extra wrap to fold over the top.
3. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food.
4. Put edges of wrap together and fold several times so paper lies directly on top of the food.
5. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
6. Label with name of food, date of freezing, and last date the food should be used for best eating quality (about 6 months).
7. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
To Heat Frozen Food:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (hot). Remove freezer wrap.
2. Place food in a baking pan and bake 1 hour or until center is hot.
